  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Digital Marketing Director",
  "description": "Dame Chops &amp; Events is a professional catering and event management company dedicated to creating memorable experiences through quality food, excellent service, and well-coordinated events. We specialize in indoor and outdoor catering, event planning, food trays, small chops, corporate events, birthdays, weddings, and special celebrations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Digital Marketing Director

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Build our digital marketing roadmap
	Manage all digital marketing channels (e.g. website, blogs, emails and social media) to ensure brand consistency
	Measure ROI and KPIs and prepare budget
	Develop specific campaigns to create and maintain high levels of customer interaction
	Oversee our social media accounts
	Manage and improve online content, considering SEO and Google Analytics
	Forecast sales performance trends and act proactively to increase our market segment
	Liaise with Product, Design and Sales directors to increase client satisfaction through cohesive strategies
	Motivate digital marketing team to achieve goals
	Monitor competition and provide ideas to stand out
	Stay up-to-date with digital technology developments.


Requirements and skills


	Proven work experience as a Digital marketing director
	Demonstrable experience in designing and implementing successful digital marketing campaigns
	Strong understanding of how all current digital marketing channels function
	Solid knowledge of online marketing tools and best practices
	Hands on experience with SEO/SEM, Google Analytics and CRM software
	Familiarity with web design
	Excellent analytical and project management skills
	Strong team management and communication (written and verbal) skills
	HND degree in Marketing, Digital technologies or relevant field.


Salary
N200,000 - N220,000 / month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: damechopsevent@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Digital Marketing Director Job at Dame Chops & Events




        
    

        
        Dame Chops & Events is a professional catering and event management company dedicated to creating memorable experiences through quality food, excellent service, and well-coordinated events. We specialize in indoor and outdoor catering, event planning, food trays, small chops, corporate events, birthdays, weddings, and special celebrations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Digital Marketing Director

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Build our digital marketing roadmap
	Manage all digital marketing channels (e.g. website, blogs, emails and social media) to ensure brand consistency
	Measure ROI and KPIs and prepare budget
	Develop specific campaigns to create and maintain high levels of customer interaction
	Oversee our social media accounts
	Manage and improve online content, considering SEO and Google Analytics
	Forecast sales performance trends and act proactively to increase our market segment
	Liaise with Product, Design and Sales directors to increase client satisfaction through cohesive strategies
	Motivate digital marketing team to achieve goals
	Monitor competition and provide ideas to stand out
	Stay up-to-date with digital technology developments.


Requirements and skills


	Proven work experience as a Digital marketing director
	Demonstrable experience in designing and implementing successful digital marketing campaigns
	Strong understanding of how all current digital marketing channels function
	Solid knowledge of online marketing tools and best practices
	Hands on experience with SEO/SEM, Google Analytics and CRM software
	Familiarity with web design
	Excellent analytical and project management skills
	Strong team management and communication (written and verbal) skills
	HND degree in Marketing, Digital technologies or relevant field.


Salary
N200,000 - N220,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior Telecom Associate Engineer",
  "description": "Western Development Company Limited (WDCL) is an independent limited liability company, established in Nigeria for over 13 years ago. WDCL is the sole representative of various overseas companies in the fields of Information Technology, Telecommunications, Mechanical Engineering and Electrical/Electronic Engineering.&nbsp;

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Senior Telecom Associate&nbsp;&nbsp;Engineer

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers

Job Summary


	Are you an experienced Telecom Engineer with a strong background in TETRA and mission-critical communication systems? Join our team and play a key role in deploying, optimizing, and maintaining communication networks that support Nigeria&rsquo;s oil &amp; gas and critical infrastructure sectors.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead the installation, configuration, and commissioning of TETRA communication systems.
	Perform RF planning, optimization, troubleshooting, and performance analysis.
	Ensure high network availability through preventive and corrective maintenance.
	Manage network monitoring, incident response, and root cause analysis.
	Configure radios, subscriber provisioning, and network security.
	Prepare technical documentation and ensure compliance with client, HSE, and NCC standards.
	Provide technical leadership, mentor junior engineers, and support client engagements.


Requirements


	B.Sc./HND in Telecommunications, Electrical/Electronic Engineering, Computer Engineering, or a related discipline.
	3&ndash;6 years of experience in telecommunications engineering.
	Vendor certifications (e.g., Motorola DIMETRA), CCNA, or RF certifications will be an advantage.
	Hands-on experience with TETRA or mission-critical radio systems.
	Strong knowledge of RF planning, troubleshooting, IP networking, microwave, and fiber transmission systems.
	Experience in the Oil &amp; Gas or critical infrastructure sector is an added advantage.

Senior Telecom Associate Engineer Job at Western Development Company Limited




        
    

        
        Western Development Company Limited (WDCL) is an independent limited liability company, established in Nigeria for over 13 years ago. WDCL is the sole representative of various overseas companies in the fields of Information Technology, Telecommunications, Mechanical Engineering and Electrical/Electronic Engineering. 

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Senior Telecom Associate  Engineer

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers

Job Summary
     

            
    




	Are you an experienced Telecom Engineer with a strong background in TETRA and mission-critical communication systems? Join our team and play a key role in deploying, optimizing, and maintaining communication networks that support Nigeria’s oil & gas and critical infrastructure sectors.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead the installation, configuration, and commissioning of TETRA communication systems.
	Perform RF planning, optimization, troubleshooting, and performance analysis.
	Ensure high network availability through preventive and corrective maintenance.
	Manage network monitoring, incident response, and root cause analysis.
	Configure radios, subscriber provisioning, and network security.
	Prepare technical documentation and ensure compliance with client, HSE, and NCC standards.
	Provide technical leadership, mentor junior engineers, and support client engagements.


Requirements


	B.Sc./HND in Telecommunications, Electrical/Electronic Engineering, Computer Engineering, or a related discipline.
	3–6 years of experience in telecommunications engineering.
	Vendor certifications (e.g., Motorola DIMETRA), CCNA, or RF certifications will be an advantage.
	Hands-on experience with TETRA or mission-critical radio systems.
	Strong knowledge of RF planning, troubleshooting, IP networking, microwave, and fiber transmission systems.
	Experience in the Oil & Gas or critical infrastructure sector is an added advantage.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at Zipline
content:
Zipline - Do you want to change the world? Zipline uses drones to deliver critical and life-saving medicine to thousands of hospitals serving millions of people in multiple countries. Our mission is to provide every human on Earth with instant access to vital medical supplies. Join Zipline and help us make this a reality for billions of people.

We are  recruiting to fill the following positions below: 

1.) Operations Readiness Program Manager

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are hiring an Operations Readiness Program Manager to lead new Long Range Platform launches across Nigeria.
	You will own country-level readiness for multiple concurrent site launches—making launch decisions, driving cross-functional alignment, and raising the operational bar so sites launch faster, safer, and scale reliably.
	This work directly enables Zipline’s mission to build an instant delivery system that delivers critical medical and commercial goods across hard-to-reach regions; your success reduces time-to-service, increases launch reliability, and ensures safe, certified flight operations in challenging field environments.


What You'll Do


	Own end-to-end operational readiness for 3–6 concurrent Long Range Platform site launches across Nigeria, from planning through first flight and early stabilization; maintain an integrated program plan with milestones, dependencies, risks, and mitigation actions.
	Define and enforce clear go/no-go criteria and measurable acceptance standards for safety, staffing, training & certification, infrastructure, equipment, communications, power, charging systems, and first-flight procedures.
	Maintain and report against launch KPIs (e.g., staffing readiness %, certification completion %, equipment acceptance pass rate, launch go/no-go rate) and use those metrics to stop or green-light launches.
	Lead the cross-functional readiness cadence and day-to-day decision rhythm, driving alignment between Recruiting, Academy, Engineering, Construction, Supply Chain, Safety/Regulatory, Partnerships, and Country Leadership; resolve conflicts and escalate issues with clear decision requests.
	Validate readiness through field verification: lead on-site checkouts, acceptance tests, and flight-readiness demonstrations; identify and close launch-critical gaps before first flight.
	Own staffing and competency readiness: confirm role fills, onboarding, and on-site proficiency; own the decision that site teams can operate independently and safely at first flight.
	Coordinate regulatory and community-readiness actions required for launch; ensure permit/approval evidence and safety artifacts meet acceptance standards before launch.
	Lead post-launch reviews and root-cause analysis, measure performance against launch targets (e.g., time-to-service, initial reliability), and convert findings into updated standards, playbooks, and training requirements.
	Escalate unresolved safety, regulatory, or critical-path blockers promptly with recommended remediation and clear authority boundaries.


What You'll Bring


	5+ years program management, field operations, or deployment experience in technical, regulated, or aviation-adjacent environments; direct site launch or activation experience required/preferred as documented in prior roles.
	Proven track record managing multi-stakeholder launches or rollouts across field sites with measurable outcomes (examples: number of sites launched, % time-to-launch reduction, reduced safety incident rate); be prepared to cite specific metrics from past programs.
	Demonstrated ability to define go/no-go criteria, write acceptance tests, and enforce KPIs under schedule pressure; comfortable making and owning launch decisions.
	Hands-on experience coordinating engineering, construction, supply chain, and training to achieve first operational use at field sites; experience validating power, communications, charging systems, aircraft, and ground equipment preferred.
	Clear escalation judgment and stakeholder management: can resolve conflicts, escalate early, and present decision-ready summaries to country leadership.
	Able to operate in high-intensity field conditions: willing to spend extended periods on-site during launches and to work irregular hours around critical milestones.
	Willingness and ability to travel throughout Nigeria :20–30% typically, with higher intensity during launch windows.
	Fluency in English and authorization to work in Nigeria.


Click here to apply online

 

2.) New Deployments Lead

Job Location: Nigeria

About You and The Role


	As the New Deployments Lead, Nigeria (senior individual contributor/people?leader), you will own end-to-end delivery and launch readiness for Zipline sites so hospitals, clinics, and health systems reliably receive life?saving products on schedule. 
	You will operate at the nexus of construction, aviation regulation, supplier networks, and in?country operations, translating Zipline’s global operational and safety standards into sites that are permitted, built, commissioned, and ready for flight operations. 
	This role is high?ownership and high?intensity: you will be accountable for delivering the country deployment plan on schedule, within budget, and to measurable reliability and quality thresholds while building and leading the Nigeria deployments organization.


What You'll Do


	Own full lifecycle delivery and formal operational handover for all new Zipline sites in Nigeria: site selection input, permitting and approvals, civil/electrical/communications construction, systems commissioning, safety acceptance, aviation clearance, and transfer to Operations.
	Manage a portfolio of deployments (up to 12 total; concurrently up to 6 active sites), maintaining an integrated program plan with clear dependencies, critical paths, resource allocations, risk register, and escalation points.
	Define, enforce, and report launch?readiness acceptance criteria for each site as binary milestones plus percent?complete against baseline (examples: permits approved, infrastructure to spec, power/comm uptime targets, systems commissioned, safety validations, aviation clearance). Drive acceptance tests and defect thresholds at handover.
	Own program outcomes and tradeoffs: schedule adherence (plan vs. actual), budget variance (% and absolute), reliability/quality metrics at handover (defect rate, commissioning pass rate), and sites launched per quarter. Authorize corrective actions and rebalancing of scope/resources to protect safety, schedule, and budget.
	Build, hire, and lead the Nigeria deployments team (project/program managers and site leads); directly manage external consultants, contractors, and on?site supervisors during execution. Clarify reporting lines and decision authority with Country and Global Leadership.
	Manage vendors and contractors end?to?end: scopes, contracts, schedules, costs, quality control, and on?site supervision of civil, electrical, and communications work. Hold vendors to measurable acceptance tests and enforce remediation for defects.
	Coordinate daily with Operations, Safety, Engineering, Supply Chain, Regulatory, and Country Leadership to resolve design, procurement, certification, and operational readiness issues that impact launch readiness.
	Own in?country deployment budgets and financial tracking; report variance explanations and corrective plans to country and global stakeholders.
	Capture lessons learned and continuously improve Zipline’s deployment playbook, SOPs, commissioning checklists, and acceptance criteria to reduce defects and accelerate future launches.


What You'll Bring


	Must be a licensed civil engineer.
	8+ years of relevant experience in construction, infrastructure delivery, engineering, or technical program management, including at least 3 years leading teams or complex programs and demonstrable hands?on field delivery experience.
	Proven track record delivering multiple technically complex infrastructure projects concurrently through permitting, commissioning, and handover; experience in Nigeria strongly preferred.
	Direct experience interacting with Nigerian regulatory bodies (aviation or infrastructure) and securing site permits/approvals for regulated projects.
	Practical, demonstrable experience managing contractors and on?site civil, electrical, and communications works and driving commissioning to measurable acceptance criteria and defect thresholds.
	Strong program management discipline: integrated schedules, resource and contingency planning, budget ownership, risk register management, and decisiveness to make program tradeoffs.
	Metrics?driven: able to define, track, and report binary launch milestones, schedule variance, budget variance, and basic reliability/quality metrics at handover.
	Comfortable operating in high?intensity, ambiguous field environments with variable site conditions and supply?chain constraints; able and willing to spend significant time on active sites.
	Excellent stakeholder management and clear written and verbal communication with technical, regulatory, community, and executive partners.
	Willingness and ability to travel frequently across Nigeria and be on?site for extended deployment phases; must be based in or able to relocate to Nigeria for the role.
	Legal authorization to work in Nigeria; Zipline may provide sponsorship where applicable.


Click here to apply online

 

 

3.) Recruiting Partner

Job Location: Lagos, Nigeria
Job Type: Full time

About You and The Role


	We are dedicated to building the strongest team in the world. We are looking for someone who enjoys partnering with hiring managers to develop thoughtful approaches to recruiting talent across Africa. Our ideal candidate is passionate about not only building a strong team, but also a team that exhibits and leads with our core values. 
	This job is not easy - nothing is a plug and play model. Each role is slightly different - we hire for new teams in new markets with dynamic timelines and hiring plans that haven’t been tested before.
	You will be the person not only to identify and recruit talent, but also the person that gives pushback to hiring teams on timelines, compensation frameworks, level of hire, and team structure.
	This role requires someone that has excellent project management, stakeholder management, and a knack for leading with authenticity. 
	Is this the kind of challenge you are looking for? Read on.


What You'll Do


	Partner with the business across Africa to recruit and close the best talent out there who share our mission
	Serve as a thought partner for our hiring managers to help them understand key information around talent pool, advertising strategy, interview plan, and more for new and recurring openings
	Monitor important data insights across the entire recruiting funnel, from sourcing to close, and continuously adjust based on insights
	Build and improve upon existing processes to ensure our recruiting processes are effective and efficient
	Strategically plan and own a pipeline of diverse, exceptionally qualified candidates and ensure a first-class experience throughout the entire process
	Help to ensure that Zipline is prioritizing diversity in all of our hiring efforts across the company 
	Creatively leverage various platforms to source and engage extraordinary talent 
	Have fun and feel energized by having a helping hand in growing Zipline and building a legendary company! 


What You'll Bring


	5+ years of high-volume, full lifecycle recruiting experience
	
		Bonus: Experience with software recruiting 
	
	
	Proven track record of sourcing and closing exceptional operational and/or technical talent 
	Strong organizational skills & ability to multitask and adapt to fast-paced startup environment
	Passion for building diverse teams & pipelines
	Creative problem-solver, going the extra mile to close the top talent and passionate about cultivating the most enjoyable candidate experience
	Willingness to invest in your own growth and become a true partner to our hiring teams
	Excellent project management and stakeholder management skills
	Expertise in tracking recruiting metrics to create data-driven strategies
	Excellent leadership, communication, and management skills
	Fluency in written and spoken English
	Bonus: Experience with Greenhouse, GEM, & Excel


Click here to apply online

Note


	Zipline is an equal opportunity employer and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws or our own sensibilities. 
	We value diversity at Zipline and welcome applications from those who are traditionally underrepresented in tech. If you like the sound of this position but are not sure if you are the perfect fit, please apply!
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Store & Logistics Officer",
  "description": "Wartsila Marine and Power Services Nigeria Limited is a global leader in smart technologies and complete lifecycle solutions for the marine and energy markets. By emphasising sustainable innovation, total efficiency and data analytics, W&auml;rtsil&auml; maximises the environmental and economic performance of the vessels and power plants of its customers. In 2018, W&auml;rtsil&auml;&rsquo;s net sales totalled EUR 5.2 billion with approximately 19,000 employees. The company has operations in over 200 locations in more than 80 countries around the world.&nbsp;

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting candidaters to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Store &amp; Logistics Officer

Job Location: Lagos

Description


	We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Store &amp; Logistics Officer to manage storekeeping, inventory control, and logistics operations at our power plant site in Victoria Island, Lagos, Nigeria.
	The role is responsible for ensuring the timely availability of spare parts, tools, and materials required for plant operations while maintaining accurate inventory records and coordinating logistics activities to support maintenance schedules and operational efficiency.
	The successful candidate will play a key role in optimizing warehouse processes, ensuring compliance with safety standards, and supporting continuous improvement initiatives.


Main Responsibilities


	Handle all plant-related storekeeping and logistics activities.
	Liaise with the PCM and logistics team on spare parts ordering, shipments, and accurate deliveries to site to meet maintenance schedule requirements.
	Receive incoming goods and verify quantities, quality, and documentation against purchase orders and invoices.
	Accurately sort, label, and store goods while optimizing warehouse space utilization.
	Pick, issue, and deliver goods according to established logistics processes.
	Maintain logistics and inventory data in relevant systems to ensure accurate and up-to-date stock records.&nbsp;
	Retrieve and provide inventory-related information such as stock transfers, inventory levels, and delivery schedules.
	Perform routine administrative activities, including updating stock records and completing receipt, transfer, delivery, and dispatch documentation.
	Plan and schedule logistics movements for the plant as directed by the Plant Manager.
	Actively participate in Continuous Improvement initiatives related to quality, safety, delivery, and operational efficiency.
	Maintain the store or warehouse in accordance with 5S principles and company safety standards.
	Coach and support colleagues in daily storekeeping and logistics activities.
	Maintain adequate inventory and records of all tools on site.
	Ensure tools are properly maintained and readily available for operational use.
	Monitor tool calibration schedules and maintain accurate calibration records.
	Communicate effectively with internal and external stakeholders to coordinate activities, resolve issues, and improve processes.
	Perform any other duties assigned by the Plant Manager.


Education &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Engineering, Supply Chain Management, Procurement, Logistics, Business Administration, or a related field.&nbsp;
	Minimum of 4 years of relevant experience in storekeeping, warehouse operations, inventory management, or logistics within a power plant, industrial, manufacturing, or heavy industry environment.&nbsp;
	Experience working with inventory management systems, ERP platforms, or warehouse management tools is an advantage.


Required Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of warehouse operations, inventory control, and logistics processes.&nbsp;
	Familiarity with spare parts management and stock optimization practices.&nbsp;
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel, and inventory management systems.&nbsp;
	Strong planning, organizational, and record-keeping skills.&nbsp;
	Ability to analyze inventory data and prepare accurate reports.&nbsp;
	Good understanding of workplace safety requirements and 5S principles.&nbsp;
	Effective communication and stakeholder management skills.
	Strong problem-solving abilities and attention to detail.&nbsp;
	Ability to&nbsp;&nbsp;work independently while collaborating effectively within a team environment.
	High level of integrity, accountability, and commitment to operational excellence.
	Pack, label, and dispatch outgoing goods with all relevant documentation.

Store & Logistics Officer Job at Wartsila Marine and Power Services Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Wartsila Marine and Power Services Nigeria Limited is a global leader in smart technologies and complete lifecycle solutions for the marine and energy markets. By emphasising sustainable innovation, total efficiency and data analytics, Wärtsilä maximises the environmental and economic performance of the vessels and power plants of its customers. In 2018, Wärtsilä’s net sales totalled EUR 5.2 billion with approximately 19,000 employees. The company has operations in over 200 locations in more than 80 countries around the world. 

We are  recruiting candidaters to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Store & Logistics Officer

Job Location: Lagos
     

            
    



Description


	We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Store & Logistics Officer to manage storekeeping, inventory control, and logistics operations at our power plant site in Victoria Island, Lagos, Nigeria.
	The role is responsible for ensuring the timely availability of spare parts, tools, and materials required for plant operations while maintaining accurate inventory records and coordinating logistics activities to support maintenance schedules and operational efficiency.
	The successful candidate will play a key role in optimizing warehouse processes, ensuring compliance with safety standards, and supporting continuous improvement initiatives.


Main Responsibilities


	Handle all plant-related storekeeping and logistics activities.
	Liaise with the PCM and logistics team on spare parts ordering, shipments, and accurate deliveries to site to meet maintenance schedule requirements.
	Receive incoming goods and verify quantities, quality, and documentation against purchase orders and invoices.
	Accurately sort, label, and store goods while optimizing warehouse space utilization.
	Pick, issue, and deliver goods according to established logistics processes.
	Maintain logistics and inventory data in relevant systems to ensure accurate and up-to-date stock records. 
	Retrieve and provide inventory-related information such as stock transfers, inventory levels, and delivery schedules.
	Perform routine administrative activities, including updating stock records and completing receipt, transfer, delivery, and dispatch documentation.
	Plan and schedule logistics movements for the plant as directed by the Plant Manager.
	Actively participate in Continuous Improvement initiatives related to quality, safety, delivery, and operational efficiency.
	Maintain the store or warehouse in accordance with 5S principles and company safety standards.
	Coach and support colleagues in daily storekeeping and logistics activities.
	Maintain adequate inventory and records of all tools on site.
	Ensure tools are properly maintained and readily available for operational use.
	Monitor tool calibration schedules and maintain accurate calibration records.
	Communicate effectively with internal and external stakeholders to coordinate activities, resolve issues, and improve processes.
	Perform any other duties assigned by the Plant Manager.


Education & Experience


	Bachelor's Degree in Engineering, Supply Chain Management, Procurement, Logistics, Business Administration, or a related field. 
	Minimum of 4 years of relevant experience in storekeeping, warehouse operations, inventory management, or logistics within a power plant, industrial, manufacturing, or heavy industry environment. 
	Experience working with inventory management systems, ERP platforms, or warehouse management tools is an advantage.


Required Skills & Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of warehouse operations, inventory control, and logistics processes. 
	Familiarity with spare parts management and stock optimization practices. 
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel, and inventory management systems. 
	Strong planning, organizational, and record-keeping skills. 
	Ability to analyze inventory data and prepare accurate reports. 
	Good understanding of workplace safety requirements and 5S principles. 
	Effective communication and stakeholder management skills.
	Strong problem-solving abilities and attention to detail. 
	Ability to  work independently while collaborating effectively within a team environment.
	High level of integrity, accountability, and commitment to operational excellence.
	Pack, label, and dispatch outgoing goods with all relevant documentation.
    

    

        Click here to Apply
